1: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was was righteous in his own eyes.
2: Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the kindred of Ram: against Job was his wrath kindled, because he justified himself rather than God.
3: Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled, because they had found no answer, and yet yet had condemned Job.
4: Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken, because they were were elder than he.
5: When Elihu saw that there was there was no answer in the mouth of these these three men, then his wrath was kindled.
6: Wherefore I was afraid, and durst not shew you mine opinion.
7: And multitude of years should teach wisdom.
8: And the inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding.
9: Neither do the aged understand judgment.
10: I also will shew mine opinion.
11: Whilst ye searched out what to say.
12: Or Or that answered his words:
13: God thrusteth him down, not man.
14: Neither will I answer him with your speeches.
16: But stood still, and and answered no more;)
17: I also will shew mine opinion.
18: The spirit within me constraineth me.
19: It is ready to burst like new bottles.
20: I will open my lips and answer.
21: Neither let me give flattering titles unto man.
22: In so doing In so doing my Maker would soon take me away.
